OK, Got it.

Kaggle Research Grant Program

Kaggle partners with academic institutions, research organizations, and nonprofits to advance state-of-the-art AI research. Our Research Grant program gives research teams essential resources to push the boundaries of what works and what doesn't work in AI. Two programs are available.

Benchmarks Resource Grant Program

Designed for researchers building novel benchmarks who need compute power and infrastructure.
Requirements
  • Must implement using the Kaggle Benchmarks SDK on kaggle.com/benchmarks
  • Commit to open-source publication

What you receive

cloudComplimentary compute quota
buildInfrastructure support
groupDirect access to Kaggle's product/engineering teams

Competitions Research Grant Program

Designed for research teams looking to host an AI competition or hackathon on Kaggle who need prize funding support. Apply to a competition grant in one of these formats:

Hackathon

If you have:
  • A clearly scoped prediction problem
  • Domain-specific criteria for what a successful evaluation looks like
  • Specifically for benchmark hackathons:
    • Clearly scoped definition of the intelligence capability you want to measure
    • Raw or partially-curated dataset as the benchmark foundation (Optional)
Participants implement benchmarks using the kaggle-benchmarks SDK and document methodology via Kaggle Writeups.Previous examples:
  • Measuring progress toward AGI - Cognitive abilities

Prediction Competition

If you have:
  • A clearly scoped prediction problem
  • A non-public dataset you are willing to publish
  • Ground truth labels for evaluating submitted models
Participants build models using Kaggle Notebooks (9 hours CPU runtime), scored on accuracy and ranked on a public leaderboard.Previous examples:
  • Recod.ai/LUC - Scientific Image Forgery Detection
  • Stanford RNA 3D Folding
  • BirdCLEF+ 2025

Eligibility and Selection Criteria

Benchmarks

For researchers working on novel benchmarks

Competitions

For institutions hosting AI competitions

What are the eligibility criteria?
Belong to a research institution, university, lab or non-profit organization.
check
check
Have a data licensing agreement that allows for open/any use/academic use.
check
check
Have a clear problem statement.
check
check
Willing to implement your benchmark with the Kaggle Benchmarks SDK.
check
Have a competition topic and dataset. (NA for hackathons)
check
Have the full dataset ready for review.
check
Have the evaluation metric available.
check
Allow the winners of your competition to publish their solutions under an open-source license.
check
What are the selection criteria?
Benefit to the Kaggle community.
check
check
Impact on the broader machine learning industry.
check
check
Dissemination of machine learning knowledge.
check
check
Novelty of the topic.
check
check
Complexity of the competition or benchmark.
check
check
Licensing of the data and winning model.
check
check
How to apply?
Apply here
Apply here

Application Timeline

Application RoundApplication OpenApplication Due DateNotification Date
Q1 2026January 28March 4March 25
Q2 2026April 1May 20June 10
Q3 2026June 15August 12September 2
Q4 2026September 7October 28December 2
Before applying, confirm you meet all eligibility criteria for the Benchmark Resource Grant Program or the Competition Grant Program.Applications submitted after the deadline will be considered in the next round. We'll contact you with selection results and next steps after reviewing your application.
Questions?
We look forward to hearing from you!
